factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is stable as the discussion does not indicate significant changes in new orders relative to revenue.

evidence.text: The Company’s critical accounting policies discussed in the Management’s Discussion and Analysis of the most recent annual report filed on Form 10-K. There have been no material changes to these policies.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging as total interest-bearing debt has decreased over the periods mentioned.

evidence.text: Total interest-bearing debt of the equipment operations was $4,804 million at January 29, 2017, compared with $4,814 million at October 30, 2016 and $4,745 million at January 31, 2016.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is deteriorating as cash and cash equivalents decreased in the first three months of 2017.

evidence.text: Cash and cash equivalents decreased $446 million during the first three months this year.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining capital expenditures as they are estimated to be approximately $600 million for the equipment operations in 2017.

evidence.text: Property and equipment cash expenditures for the equipment operations in the first three months of 2017 were $155 million, compared with $140 million in the same period last year. Capital expenditures for the equipment operations in 2017 are estimated to be approximately $600 million.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable as the discussion does not indicate significant wage inflation or benefit expenses creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: The Company’s critical accounting policies discussed in the Management’s Discussion and Analysis of the most recent annual report filed on Form 10-K. There have been no material changes to these policies.
